Name: Find
Effect: Opens a search box which you can use to find text in the current document.

Name: Shuffle
Effect: Hold COMMAND, and tap TAB: The first tab tap will bring up a small window that shows all open applications, another tap will highlight the next app, and the highlighted app will be brought to front when you release the keys.

Name: Save
Effect: Generally accepted command to save your progress is a document.

Name: Cut
Effect: Pastes whatever has been copied into your clipboard onto the document.

Name: Screenshot
Effect: Takes a screenshot of a selected area of your window. The cursor will change to look like a target vector, click hold and drag the cursor so the shadow covers the area you want to screenshot. Release the mouse to take the screenshot. Press the escape key to get back to your cursor.

Name: Screenshot
Effect: Takes a screenshot of your entire window, generally saves to Desktop, but also check Downloads.

Name: Paste
Effect: After selecting/highlighting the desired text, will delete the text and copy it to your clipboard.

Name: Spotlight
Effect: The Spotlight search will allow you to search for applications or documents on your machine.

Name: Tab
Effect: Opens a new tab of the active application.

Name: Switch
Effect: Moves to another desktop display if you have one; the equivalent of: three-finger swipe left or right on the trackpad.

Name: Copy
Effect: After selecting/highlighting desired text, this command will copy the text to your clipboard.

Name: Undo
Effect: Steps backwards in your keystroke history, generally one character at a time.

Name: Quit
Effect: Closes and quits the active application.

Name: Window
Effect: Closes the current window of the active application.

Name: Spread
Effect: Spreads out all the open applications on that desktop; the equivalent of: F3, or three-finger swipe up on the trackpad.

Name: New
Effect: Opens a new window of the active application.

Name: Redo
Effect: Steps forward in your keystroke history.
